questions.question: A prospect replies: 'Not interested right now, maybe next quarter.' What's the correct classification and action?

options.text: SOFT_OBJECTION — Tag as nurture, add to 90-day re-engagement

questions.explanation: This is a soft objection (timing issue, not a hard no). Tag for nurture and re-engage in 90 days. Don't push now.

questions.question: Your AI classification accuracy drops from 95% to 78% over 2 months. What's the most likely cause?

options.text: Your prompt needs retraining with new examples
The AI model was updated and changed behavior
Your reply volume increased and the AI can't handle it

questions.explanation: Classification drift happens when your prospect language changes but your prompt doesn't. Retrain with recent examples to restore accuracy.

questions.question: You're running email + LinkedIn sequences. A prospect replies on LinkedIn. What should happen to the email sequence?

options.text: Continue the email sequence — they're separate channels
Pause the email sequence for 7 days, then resume
Send one more email acknowledging their LinkedIn reply

questions.explanation: Stop all sequences immediately when someone replies on any channel. Continuing feels spammy and wastes touches.

questions.question: What's the maximum acceptable response time for a 'POSITIVE' classified reply?
questions.explanation: Aim for <4 hours on positive replies. Research shows response time dramatically impacts qualification rates. Within 1 hour is ideal but unrealistic for solo founders.

questions.question: Which reply type is SAFE to auto-respond to?
questions.explanation: Out-of-office is the only truly safe auto-response. Everything else benefits from human context and personalization.
